methods.name: checkPermission
methods.docs: Retrieves the current permission state for a given scope.

methods.docs: Retrieves the current state of all permissions.

methods.name: requestPermission

tags.name: deprecated
tags.text:
Use {@link requestWriteOnlyCalendarAccess}, {@link requestReadOnlyCalendarAccess},
{@link requestFullCalendarAccess} or {@link requestFullRemindersAccess} instead.

methods.docs: Requests permission for a given scope.

methods.name: requestAllPermissions
methods.docs: Requests permission for all calendar and reminder permissions.

methods.name: requestWriteOnlyCalendarAccess
methods.docs: Requests write access to the calendar.

methods.name: requestReadOnlyCalendarAccess
methods.docs: Requests read access to the calendar.

methods.name: requestFullCalendarAccess
methods.docs: Requests read and write access to the calendar.

methods.name: requestFullRemindersAccess
methods.docs: Requests read and write access to the reminders.

methods.name: createEventWithPrompt
methods.signature: (options?: CreateEventWithPromptOptions | undefined) => Promise<{ id: string | null; }>

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Opens the system calendar interface to create a new event.
On Android always returns `null`.
Fetch the events to find the ID of the newly created event.

methods.name: modifyEventWithPrompt

tags.name: example

methods.docs:
Opens a system calendar interface to modify an event.
On Android always returns `null`.

properties.name: alerts

tags.name: example

properties.docs:
Alert times in minutes relative to the event start.
Use negative numbers for reminders before the start, and positive numbers for reminders after the start.
On iOS only 2 alerts are supported.

properties.name: invitees
properties.docs: An array of emails to invite.

properties.name: recurrence
properties.docs: Rules for creating a recurring event.

properties.name: byMonth
properties.docs:
Limits a yearly recurrence to specific months of the year.
The values should be between 1 and 12.

properties.name: byMonthDay
properties.docs:
Limits a monthly recurrence to specific days of the month.
The values should be between 1 and 31.

properties.name: byWeekDay
properties.docs:
Limits a weekly recurrence to specific weekdays.
The values should be between 1 and 7.
1 means Monday and 7 means Sunday.

properties.name: count
properties.docs:
The total number of occurrences.
If set, the recurrence ends after this many occurrences.
If `count` is provided, `end` is ignored.

properties.name: daysOfTheYear
properties.docs: Limits a yearly recurrence to specific days of the year (1 to 366).

properties.name: end

tags.name: example

properties.docs: End date of the recurrence series as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

properties.name: frequency
properties.docs: How often the event repeats.

properties.name: interval
properties.docs:
The interval between recurrences. Use in combination with `frequency`.
For example, a weekly event with an interval of 2, results in the event
occurring every 2 weeks.

properties.name: weeksOfTheYear
properties.docs: Limits a yearly recurrence to specific ISO week numbers (1 to 53).

properties.name: alerts

properties.docs:
Alert times in minutes relative to the event start.
Use negative numbers for reminders before the start, and positive numbers for reminders after the start.
On iOS only 2 alerts are supported.

properties.type: { email: string | null; name: string | null; role: AttendeeRole | null; status: AttendeeStatus | null; type: AttendeeType | null; }[]

properties.name: from
properties.docs: The timestamp in milliseconds.

properties.name: to
properties.docs: The timestamp in milliseconds.

properties.name: internalTitle
properties.docs: Internal name of the calendar (`CalendarContract.Calendars.NAME`).

properties.name: visible
properties.docs: Indicates if the events from this calendar should be shown.

properties.name: accountName
properties.docs: The account under which the calendar is registered.

properties.name: ownerAccount
properties.docs: The owner of the calendar.

properties.name: maxReminders
properties.docs: Maximum number of reminders allowed per event.

properties.name: multiple
properties.docs: Allow multiple selections.

properties.name: color
properties.docs:
The color of the calendar.
Should be provided on Android.

properties.name: accountName
properties.docs: Only needed on Android. Typically set to an email address.

properties.name: ownerAccount
properties.docs: Only needed on Android. Typically set to an email address.

properties.name: id
properties.docs: Identifier of the newly created reminders list.

properties.name: color
properties.docs: The color of the list.

properties.name: commit
properties.docs:
Whether to save the list to the event store immediately.
Pass `false` to batch multiple changes and commit them together using `CapacitorCalendar.commit()`, which is more efficient than committing each save individually.

properties.name: sourceId
properties.docs:
The EKSource identifier (account) where the list should be created.
If left undefined, iCloud will be used if available, otherwise falls back to local.

properties.name: title
properties.docs: The title of the list.

properties.name: commit
properties.docs:
Whether to save the deletion to the event store immediately.
Pass `false` to batch multiple changes and commit them together using `CapacitorCalendar.commit()`, which is more efficient than committing each save individually.

properties.name: id
properties.docs: Identifier of the reminders list to delete.

properties.name: alerts

tags.name: example

properties.docs:
Alert times in minutes relative to the reminder start.
Use negative numbers for alerts before the start, and positive numbers for alerts after the start.

properties.name: interval
properties.docs: How often it repeats (e.g. 1 for every occurrence, 2 for every second occurrence).

properties.name: end
properties.docs: Timestamp of when the recurrence ends.

properties.name: alerts

tags.name: example

properties.docs:
Alert times in minutes relative to the reminder start.
Use negative numbers for alerts before the start, and positive numbers for alerts after the start.

On iOS only 2 alerts are supported.

properties.name: title
properties.docs: Title of the dialog.

properties.name: message
properties.docs: Message of the dialog.

properties.name: confirmButtonText
properties.docs: Text to show on the confirm button.

properties.name: cancelButtonText
properties.docs: Text to show on the cancel button.

properties.name: id
properties.docs: Identifier of the updated reminders list.

properties.name: color
properties.docs:
The new color of the list.
If omitted, the color is left unchanged.

properties.name: commit
properties.docs:
Whether to save the update to the event store immediately.
Pass `false` to batch multiple changes and commit them together using `eventStore.commit()`, which is more efficient than committing each save individually.

properties.name: id
properties.docs: The identifier of the list to update.

properties.name: title
properties.docs:
The new title of the list.
If omitted, the title is left unchanged.

members.docs: Permission required for adding or modifying calendar events.

members.docs: Permission required for adding or modifying reminders.
